title: Ecteinanthus ovatus
intro: Ecteinanthus ovatus, also known as the Ecteinanthus plant, is a tropical plant species native to the rainforests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in relieving pain and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Ecteinanthus ovatus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ecteinanthus ovatus is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction to treat various ailments, although its efficacy and safety need further scientific investigation.

Its leaves and stems are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
